sections.paragraph:
Συχέμ (שְׁכֶם) indecl.

① fem., Shechem (Συχέμ TestLevi 5:3=Σίκιμα Gen 48:22; Josh 24:32; Demetr.: 722 Fgm. 1, 8 Jac.; Theodotus [II b.c.]: 732 Fgm. 1 p. 692, 23 al. Jac. [in Eus., PE 9, 22, 2]; Joseph.), a city in Samaria, destroyed 128 b.c. by Hyrcanus I. West of it Vespasian founded a new city, Flavia Neapolis (Eus., Onom. p. 150, 1) Ac 7:16ab.—KBaedeker, Pal. u. Syr.7 1910, 203ff; FFörster, Sichem, s. Gesch. u. Bed., diss. Leipzig 1923; various articles ZDPV 1926–28; ESellin (earlier material in PThomsen, Palätina u. s. Kultur3 ’31, 116): ZAW 50, ’32, 303–8; PThomsen, Reallex. d. Vorgesch. XII 1928, 74ff; TBöhl, De opgraving van Sichem 1927; GWright in BASOR ’56–’62, The Biography of a Bibl. City ’65; ENielsen, Shechem, A Traditio-Hist. Investigation ’55; BHHW III, 1781–83; Kl. Pauly V 163.

② masc., a son of Hamor, Shechem (s. Ἑμμώρ) Ἑμμὼρ τοῦ Συχέμ (cp. Gen 33:19; JosAs 23:13) Hamor the father of Shechem Ac 7:16 v.l. (in Jos., Ant. 1, 337f Συχέμμης).—M-M. EDNT.

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

fem. fem. = feminine

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Theodotus Theodotus before I b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

West West = MWest, Iambi et Elegi Graeci—List 5

Syr. Syr. = Syriac

diss. diss. = dissertation

ZDPV ZDPV = Zeitschrift der Deutschen Palästina-Vereins—List 6

ZAW ZAW = Zeitschrift für die Alttestamentliche Wissenschaft—Lists 6

BASOR BASOR = Bulletin of the American Schools of Oriental Research—List 6

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

Kl. Pauly Kl. Pauly = Der Kleine Pauly: Lexikon der Antike—List 6

masc. masc. = masculine

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

EDNT EDNT = Exegetical Dictionary of the NT—List 6
